subject: [f2fs-dev] [PATCH v2 17/18] iomap: pass iomap_next_fn directly instead of struct iomap_ops

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2.

2026-06-30.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the iomap API by removing the struct iomap_ops and passing the iomap_next function directly to various functions. This change is possible because all filesystems have implemented the ->iomap_next() method, eliminating the need for the legacy fallback methods. The patch makes no functional changes but reduces code complexity and improves maintainability.

reviews.author: Jan Kara
reviews.summary: Reviewer Jan Kara noted that the patch does not include Acked-by tags for ext2 and ext4 filesystems, which are required for these changes.
reviews.raw_body:
Feel free to add:

Acked-by: Jan Kara <jack@suse.cz>

for ext2 & ext4 changes.

								Honza

2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the iomap API by removing the struct iomap_ops and passing the iomap_next function directly to various functions. This change is possible because all filesystems have implemented the ->iomap_next() method, eliminating the need for the legacy fallback methods. The patch makes no functional changes but reduces code complexity and improves maintainability.

reviews.author: Christoph Hellwig
reviews.summary: Christoph Hellwig suggested delaying the patch and a previous one to the next merge window to minimize cross-subsystem merge pain due to file system iomap conversions.
reviews.raw_body:
Looks good:

Reviewed-by: Christoph Hellwig <hch@lst.de>

In terms of merge logistics, I wonder if we should delay this and
the previous patch to the next merge window so that we can minimize the
cross-subsystem merge pain with more file system iomap conversion.
If none of them actually happen until rc6 or so, orif  the merges aren't
painful we could still pick them up late in the merge window.

reviews.author: Darrick Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary: The reviewer suggested that all patches but this one should be merged during the merge window for 7.3, and requested specific instructions to ensure timely inclusion of this patch.
reviews.raw_body:
I'd say everything but this patch should go in during the merge window
for 7.3, along with clear instructions to brauner/torvalds to expect
this patch to appear right before 7.3-rc1 gets tagged, to clean up all
the other changes that come in.

--D

reviews.author: Darrick Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Darrick suggested renaming the iomap_next_fn parameter to iter_fn, aligning it with the earlier proposed rename of the typedef from struct iomap_ops to iomap_iter_fn.

The reviewer questioned the clarity of the patch description, specifically the use of 'iomap_next' in its own definition, and requested a more descriptive title.

The reviewer suggested renaming the iomap_next_fn parameter to a more descriptive name, such as iter_fn, to better reflect its purpose and avoid confusion with struct iomap_ops.

Reviewer noted that the patch looks solid aside from some minor naming issues, referred to as 'bikeshedding', and expressed no further concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
If you took my earlier suggestion to rename the typedef to
iomap_iter_fn, then this parameter ought to be named iter_fn.

---

"The iomap iteration function for the filesystem" ?

Using the term "iomap_next" in the definition for iomap_next isn't that
helpful.

---

Probably ought to be called iter_fn, or at least something that isn't
"dops".

---

Aside from the name bikeshedding, the logic looks solid. :)

--D


_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary:
Author suggests renaming the argument 'iter_fn' to a more descriptive name 'iter', implying no major changes are needed.

Author acknowledged a naming issue and agreed to rename the function in the next version of the patch.
reviews.raw_body:
Hmm... maybe at that point, it's self-explanatory enough that the arg
could just be called "iter" instead of "iter_fn"?

---

Nice spotting, I'll rename this in the next version.

Thanks,
Joanne


_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

reviews.author: Darrick Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Darrick Linux-f2fs-devel suggested renaming iomap_next_fn to iter_fn for clarity, citing existing variables named 'iter' that represent iteration state objects.

Reviewer noted that the patch does not handle the case where iomap_next_fn is NULL, and requested a check to be added before calling it.

The reviewer pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where iomap_next_fn is NULL, which could lead to a null pointer dereference.
reviews.raw_body:
Dunno.  Seeing as we already have variables named "iter" that are the
actual iteration state object, I think it's clearer to leave the
iteration function as "iter_fn".

---

<nod>

--D

---

_______________________________________________

https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author clarified that she interpreted reviewer's concern as wanting to keep ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() functions available for other filesystems, and agreed to submit a revised patch set in v3, excluding the current patch and the previous one.
reviews.raw_body:
Just to clarify, did you mean this patch and the previous one? If i'm
interpreting Christoph's concern correctly, I think he's worried about
other filesystems converting to iomap using the ->iomap_begin() /
->iomap_end() functions still? That sounds like a good plan to me, for
v3 I'll submit everything but this patch and the last one and then
submit these patches (and any cleanup ones that become necessary) to
Christian right before 7.3-rc1 gets tagged (which as I understand it,
is when the merge window is about to close).

Thanks,
Joanne


_______________________________________________
Linux-f2fs-devel mailing list
Linux-f2fs-devel@lists.sourceforge.net
https://lists.sourceforge.net/lists/listinfo/linux-f2fs-devel

reviews.author: Darrick Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary:
The reviewer expressed concern that the patch does not consider potential issues related to filesystems that may still use struct iomap_ops, and suggested obtaining approval from all affected parties beforehand.

Reviewer Darrick pointed out that the patch does not handle the case where iomap_next_fn is NULL, which could lead to a null pointer dereference in iomap_iter().
reviews.raw_body:
Yes.  And be sure to ask both of them beforehand so there aren't any
youknowwho-style surprises/outrages.

--D

---

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch simplifies the iomap API by removing the struct iomap_ops and passing the iomap_next function directly to various functions. This change is possible because all filesystems have implemented the ->iomap_next() method, eliminating the need for the legacy fallback methods. The patch makes no functional changes but reduces code complexity and improves maintainability.

reviews.author: Christian Linux-f2fs-devel
reviews.summary:
reviewer noted that passing iomap_next_fn directly could lead to issues if filesystems need to implement different behavior for ->iomap_next() and suggested adding a check to ensure the function pointer is not NULL

The reviewer noted that the patch description mentions 'No functional change intended', but they requested to merge these changes after v7.3-rc1 has been tagged, implying a potential conflict or dependency on this specific version.
sentiment_signals: potential issue with NULL function pointer
reviews.raw_body:
Ok, so we'll do the prep for vfs-7.3.iomap (aka to be merged in the
v7.3-rc1 cycle)...

---

and merge these _after_ v7.3-rc1 has been tagged...
